system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    (20  0   1)    // 5
    (20  20  1)    // 6
    ( 0  20  1)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      chemistryProperties;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          standard;
}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='combustionProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

combustionModel  laminar;

active          true;

laminarCoeffs

}

{
    version     2.0;

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='reactions', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      reactions;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);

reactions
{
    methaneReaction
    {
        type        irreversibleArrheniusReaction;
        reaction    "CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O";
        A           5.012e11;
        beta        0;
        Ta          24358;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='thermo.compressibleGas', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

    transport       sutherland;

foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";

keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/2/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/2/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 30.

<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same error as Attempts 3, 4, and 5. Despite multiple attempts to fix the `physicalProperties` file by various approaches (inline species sub-dictionaries, separate species files, cleaning legacy entries), the error persists. This means the fundamental approach is incorrect for this version of OpenFOAM.

## Root Cause

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file more carefully, I can see it already has the correct structure from the previous attempt (Attempt 5): `multiComponentMixture` mixture type, `inertSpecie N2;`, `species` list, and inline species sub-dictionaries with `MW`. Yet the error still says `CH4` is undefined.

The key insight is that in **OpenFOAM 10** (Foundation version), the `multiComponentMixture` expects the species property dictionaries to be found through a **different mechanism**. The solver is looking for `CH4` as a **sub-dictionary** inside `physicalProperties`, but it's possible the parsing or loading mechanism is failing.

However, given that this error has persisted across 5 attempts with variations of the same approach, I need to take a completely different direction.

## Alternative Approach

In OpenFOAM 10, the species property files may need to be discovered via the **reactions file** using the `chemistryReader` mechanism, rather than being embedded in `physicalProperties`. Even though earlier attempts said to remove the chemistry reader directives, perhaps they need to be **added back** alongside the `multiComponentMixture` approach.

But since the previous attempt specifically warned about removing those directives... wait, let me re-examine the current `physicalProperties`. Actually, looking at it now, it **does NOT** contain the legacy chemistry reader directives (`chemistryReader`, `foamChemistryFile`, `foamChemistryThermoFile`). Those were suggested for removal in earlier attempts but may be REQUIRED by this version of OpenFOAM.

## Solution

### 1. Add the legacy chemistry reader directives to `constant/physicalProperties`

Add the following entries to `constant/physicalProperties`:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;

foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";

foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";
```

These tell OpenFOAM 10 to read the species list, reactions, and thermodynamic data from the `reactions` file and associated thermo file, rather than trying to parse them from `physicalProperties` directly.

### 2. Keep the existing `physicalProperties` content unchanged

Do NOT remove the `species` list, `inertSpecie N2;`, or the inline species sub-dictionaries. Keep `multiComponentMixture` as the mixture type. The chemistry reader will populate the mixture from the reactions file, and the species list/inertSpecie in `physicalProperties` serves as a fallback or reference.

### 3. Verify `thermo.compressibleGas` exists

Ensure the file `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` exists. Based on the provided files, it should already be there. If not, create it with appropriate species thermodynamic data (or it may be auto-generated from the reactions file with the chemistry reader).

### Summary

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | Add the three chemistry reader directives: `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;`, `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";`, `foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";` |

This approach uses the modern OpenFOAM 10 chemistry framework (reading from `reactions` file) while preserving the `multiComponentMixture` setup already in place. The chemistry reader will handle populating the species properties, which should resolve the `CH4 is undefined` error.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.2 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/physicalProperties', changes='Add chemistryReader foamChemistryReader; after inertSpecie line; Add foamChemistryFile entry pointing to <constant>/reactions; Add foamChemistryThermoFile entry pointing to <constant>/thermo.compressibleGas')]
